113:15752.name: Perosonal heroes in teamfights analysis detailed panel
113:15752.characters: Perosonal heroes in teamfights analysis detailed panel

113:15757.name: Battle map of teamfights heroes in teamfights analysis detailed panel
113:15757.characters: Battle map of teamfights heroes in teamfights analysis detailed panel

114:15881.name: Users can jump to the new web interface to check more detailed teamfights analysis data through button-more of the rapid panel. This web interface is divided into 2 modules, battle map, and hero analysis. Compared with the teamfights rapid analysis panel, users can view more detailed data and more filtering rights, which are suitable for scenarios that require in-depth analysis.
114:15881.characters: Users can jump to the new web interface to check more detailed teamfights analysis data through button-more of the rapid panel. This web interface is divided into 2 modules, battle map, and hero analysis. Compared with the teamfights rapid analysis panel, users can view more detailed data and more filtering rights, which are suitable for scenarios that require in-depth analysis.

114:15868.name: The record reminder will pop out in the interface of the game live when after the system algorithm completes the data collation of teamfights. The pop-up box will last for three seconds and then shrink into the button of the teamfight record. The user can view the teamfights analysis panel while watching the live broadcast. This function satisfies the audience's need to view the data of teamfights in real-time. It is convenient for users to autonomously view the data of teamfights they want to know. After clicking on the button, the teamfights rapid analysis panel will pop up from the right side of the live interface. This panel will mainly show the death analysis, because according to the user research above, users are most concerned about the damage of heroes in teamfights, and the damage mainly revolves around the dead heroes. Therefore, I design three main function modules to present death analysis data. They are as follows. 1. the battle map of death heroes 2. personal data of death heroes 3. the debuff timeline of death heroes The following sections will show the prototype and analysis of these modules in turn.
114:15868.characters:

The following sections will show the prototype and analysis of these modules in turn.

110:14505.name: The Dotabuff Combat: The scope stretches over the whole game and thus the focus is not on isolated Teamfights, but the information is offered in finer granularity too by e.g. distinguishing between damage types and giving information about Crowd Control.

111:14950.name: He usually watches esports to improve his game skills. When watching live streams, he gets to know the match process by listening to the commentator and looking at the game panel. Sometimes he doesn't understand exactly what the commentator is talking about and will turn on the analytics platform but the data of teamfights part on the platform is very limited. He can't learn more about the events that happen at more detailed times. And what abilities and items do individual heroes apply to increase damage during the teamfights.
111:14950.characters: He usually watches esports to improve his game skills. When watching live streams, he gets to know the match process by listening to the commentator and looking at the game panel. Sometimes he doesn't understand exactly what the commentator is talking about and will turn on the analytics platform but the data of teamfights part on the platform is very limited. He can't learn more about the events that happen at more detailed times. And what abilities and items do individual heroes apply to increase damage during the teamfights.

110:14483.name: It shows how much damage was dealt to a hero as well as its source (standard attack, ability, item) and what status effects were involved.
110:14483.characters: It shows how much damage was dealt to a hero as well as its source (standard attack, ability, item) and what status effects were involved.

114:15867.name: The record reminder will pop out in the interface of the game live when after the system algorithm completes the data collation of teamfights. The pop-up box will last for three seconds and then shrink into the button of the teamfight record. The user can view the teamfights analysis panel while watching the live broadcast. This function satisfies the audience's need to view the data of teamfights in real-time. It is convenient for users to autonomously view the data of teamfights they want to know. After clicking on the button, the teamfights rapid analysis panel will pop up from the right side of the live interface. This panel will mainly show the death analysis, because according to the user research above, users are most concerned about the damage of heroes in teamfights, and the damage mainly revolves around the dead heroes. Therefore, I design three main function modules to present death analysis data. They are as follows. 1. the battle map of death heroes 2. personal data of death heroes 3. the debuff timeline of death heroes The following sections will show the prototype and analysis of these modules in turn. Users can jump to the new web interface to check more detailed teamfights analysis data through button-more of the rapid panel. This web interface is divided into 2 modules, battle map, and hero analysis. Compared with the teamfights rapid analysis panel, users can view more detailed data and more filtering rights, which are suitable for scenarios that require in-depth analysis.

110:14458.name: The Dota2 Rumble Flow is an interactive graph-based visualization that supports Teamfights analysis. It does not replace conventional Teamfights summaries, but instead benefits from them as they provide more information and context.

110:14453.name: The Dotabuff Combat: The scope stretches over the whole game and thus the focus is not on isolated Teamfights, but the information is offered in finer granularity too by e.g. distinguishing between damage types and giving information about Crowd Control.
